alarm-type:sr_io_throughput_total_per_ho: SR IO throughput total per host exceeds @:n-percent
allow-self-signed-ssl: You may need to allow self-signed SSL certificates in your browser
aria:breadcrumb:label: Breadcrumb
attach-vdi:error-message: An error occurred while attaching the VDI. Please review your configuration settings (SR, VDI, mode), verify the VM state, and try again.
back-pool-dashboard: Go back to your Pool dashboard
back-site-dashboard: Go back to your site dashboard
backups:jobs:issues-ran-without-hitch: All your backup jobs ran without a hitch.
backups:vms-protection:active-protected: In at least 1 active job & protected
backups:vms-protection:active-unprotecte: In at least 1 active job but unprotected
backups:vms-protection:tooltip: A VM is protected if it's in a backup job, with an enabled schedule, and the last run succeeded
backups:vms-protection:unprotected: In at least 1 job but unprotected
cbt-destroy-snapshot-data: Purge snapshot data when using CBT
click-to-return-default-pool: Click here to return to the default pool
compression-not-available: Compression is not available for SRs from the same pool.
configure-for-protected: To ensure your VMs protection, consider configuring a {backup-job}
confirm-cancel?: Are you sure you want to cancel?
console-unavailable-reason: The console is currently unavailable because the {type} is powered off.
copy-host-bios-strings: Copy host BIOS strings to VM (only for BIOS VM).
copy-unavailable-http: Copy is not supported in HTTP context
cpu-provisioning-warning: The number of vCPUs allocated exceeds the number of physical CPUs available. System performance could be affected
deploy-xoa-available-on-desktop: XOA deployment is available on your desktop interface
disconnect-vifs-in-xo-5-to-delete-networ: To delete a network, you must first disconnect all VIFs attached to it. You can manage your VMs' VIFs in {xo-5}.
do-you-have-needs: You have needs and/or expectations? Let us know
do-you-really-want-to-leave-this-page?: Some changes are still being deployed. Do you really want to leave this page?
duplicate-vm:error-message: An error occurred while duplicating the VM. Please review your configuration settings (name, storage repository, compression) and try again.
export-vms-manually-information: Some VM exports were not able to start automatically, probably due to your browser settings. To export them, you should click on each one. (Alternatively, copy the link as well.)
fast-clone-available: Fast clone is available only for halted VMs.
field:exceeds-max-characters: Field value must be {max} characters or less.
hide-successful-items: Hide successful items in failure reports
host-currently-shutdown: This host is currently shut down, so we can’t retrieve any data.
info:vdi-attached-rw: This VDI is already attached in read-write mode to another VM.
invalid-ip-addresses: One or more invalid IP addresses
ip-address-info: You can apply a rule to a single IP address or a subnet in CIDR format. Examples: 192.168.1.10, or 10.20.0.0/16
is-part-of-one-active-job: This VM is currently part of at least one active job. The last job it participated in will appear here once it has been executed.
job:arg:pif-ids-required: At least one PIF ID is required
job:arg:port-required-for-tcp-udp: Port is required for TCP and UDP
job:network-delete:has-n-physical-pif-co: The network has one physical PIF connected | The network has {n} physical PIFs connected
job:network-delete:has-n-vif-attached: The network has one VIF attached | The network has {n} VIFs attached
job:pbd-plug:pbd-connected: One or several PBDs are already connected
job:pbd-unplug:pbd-disconnected: One or several PBDs are already disconnected
job:vbd-delete:vbd-attached: Cannot delete a VBD that is currently attached
job:vdi-delete:vbd-attached: Cannot delete a VDI with VBD attached
job:vm-export:not-allowed: A VM is not allowed to export
job:vm-force-reboot:bad-power-state: VM must be running or paused
job:vm-force-shutdown:bad-power-state: VM must be running, suspended, or paused
job:vm-resume:bad-power-state: VM must be paused or suspended
keep-page-open: Do not refresh or quit tab before end of deployment.
last-n-backup-archives: Last backup archive | Last {n} backup archives
last-n-backup-runs: Last backup run | Last {n} backup runs
leave-page-safely: You can safely leave this page.
login-only-on-master: Login is only possible on the master host
manage-citrix-pv-drivers-via-windows-upd: Manage citrix PV drivers via Windows Update
modal:confirm-host-disable: Are you sure you want to disable {host}?
modal:confirm-host-enable: Are you sure you want to enable {host}?
modal:host-disable-message: The host will be marked unavailable for starting or receiving new VMs. Already-running VMs are not affected.
modal:host-enable-message: The host becomes available again for starting or receiving VMs.
modal:vm-force-reboot-message: This will immediately reboot the virtual machine without a clean shutdown. Data loss may occur and ongoing processes will be interrupted.
modal:vm-force-shutdown-message: This will immediately power off the virtual machine without a clean shutdown. Data loss may occur and ongoing processes will be interrupted.
modal:vm-reboot-message: Restarts the virtual machine gracefully by shutting it down and starting it again. Running processes will be closed properly.
modal:vm-shutdown-message: This will gracefully shut down the virtual machine. All running processes will stop, and users may be disconnected. You can restart the VM later from the interface.
n-hosts-awaiting-patch: {n} host is awaiting this patch | {n} hosts are awaiting this patch
name: Name
new-network:creation-in-progress-message: The task triggered by the new network form will still run and can be tracked from the {tasksPageLink}.
new-network:error-message: An error occurred while creating the network. Please review your configuration settings (name, type, VLAN), verify host availability, and try again.
new-traffic-rule:error-message: An error occurred while creating the traffic rule. Please review your configuration settings and try again.
new-vdi:error-message: An error occurred while creating the VDI. Please review your configuration settings (name, storage repository, allocated space) and try again.
new-vif:error-message: An error occurred while creating the VIF. Please review your configuration settings and try again.
new-vm:name: VM name
new-vm:network-config: Network configuration is only compatible with {noCloudLink}.
new-vm:user-config-variables-escape: Escape any variable with a preceding backslash '\'
new-vm:user-config-variables-index: {'{index}'}: the VM's index, it will take 0 in case of single VM
new-vm:user-config-variables-name: {'{name}'}: the VM's name. It must not contain '_'
no-active-backup-jobs: There are no active backup jobs on this site.
no-job-vm: This VM isn’t part of any running job.
no-replicated-vm: This VM isn’t being replicated. Configure a replication job to enable it.
no-selected-vm-can-be-exported: No selected VM can be exported
no-selected-vm-can-be-migrated: No selected VM can be migrated
page-not-found: This page is not to be found…
page-please-wait: Hold tight, astronaut! The page is getting ready to take off.{newline}We're just aligning the thrusters and calibrating the systems. It won't be long now, stay tuned!
please-confirm-to-continue: This action cannot be undone. Are you sure you want to continue?
pool-connection-error-auth-failed: An error occurred while connecting to the pool. The username or password appears to be incorrect.
pool-connection-error-duplicate: An error occurred while connecting to the pool. This pool seems to be already connected.
pool-connection-error-host-not-found: An error occurred while connecting to the pool. The address of this pool does not seem to exist. Please change the IP and try again.
pool-connection-error-invalid-parameters: An error occurred while connecting to the pool. The parameters provided do not seem valid. Please check the IP address and credentials, then try again.
pool-connection-ip-info: Provide the primary host’s address of the pool you want to connect to.
pool-connection-success: You have successfully connected to the pool. You can now find it in your treeview and start managing your hosts and VMs.
pool-disconnect-info: You will no longer be able to manage this pool from XO. You can reconnect it later.
pool:status:unreachable:tooltip: Configured servers that cannot be reached
popup-first-connection-default-interface: From now on, this new version will be your default interface. If you prefer to keep XO 5 as your default for now, you can {documentationLink} to do so.
popup-first-connection-early-release: Please note that this is an early release of an ongoing project. You’ll notice that some familiar pages from XO 5 are not yet available and that most features are currently read-only.
popup-first-connection-feedback: Before we move forward, we’d love to hear your {feedbackLink} on the current state of XO 6. You can switch back to the previous version anytime by clicking the “XO 5” link in the top-right corner of any page.
popup-first-connection-introduction: We’re excited to introduce the first official release of Xen Orchestra 6.
popup-first-connection-upcoming-release: In the upcoming releases, we’ll be adding actions on all objects, user management, backup features, and much more. We hope you’ll enjoy exploring this brand-new interface!
power-on-host-for-console: Power on your host to access its console
power-on-vm-for-console: Power on your VM to access its console
query-builder:label: Search Engine
query-search-bar:label: @:query-builder:label
snapshot-delete-warning: Deleting 1 snapshot may lead to irreversible data loss. This action is permanent and cannot be undone. | Deleting {n} snapshots at once may lead to irreversible data loss. This action is permanent and cannot be undone.
snapshot-revert-snapshot-before: Take a snapshot of the VM before reverting
snapshot-revert-warning: This will restore the VM to the selected snapshot state. Any changes made since then (data, configuration, and disk state) will be lost. This action cannot be undone.
sr-connect-info-host: The SR will be connected to this host. | The selected SRs will be connected to this host.
sr-connect-info-pool-local: The SR will be connected to its host. | Each SR will be connected to its own host.
sr-connect-info-pool-mixed: Local SRs will be connected to their respective hosts. Shared SRs will be connected to all hosts in this pool.
sr-connect-info-pool-shared: The SR will be connected to {hostsCount} hosts in this pool. | Each shared SR will be connected to all hosts in this pool.
sr-connect-title: You are about to connect 1 SR | You are about to connect {n} SRs
sr-delete-info: This action will permanently delete the storage repository and all data stored on it. This cannot be undone. | All selected storage repositories and the data they contain will be permanently deleted. This action is irreversible—proceed with caution.
sr-disconnect-info-host: The SR will be disconnected from this host. | The selected SRs will be disconnected from this host.
sr-disconnect-info-pool-local: The SR will be disconnected from its host. | Each SR will be disconnected from its own host.
sr-disconnect-info-pool-mixed: Local SRs will be disconnected from their respective hosts. Shared SRs will be disconnected from all hosts in this pool.
sr-disconnect-info-pool-shared: The SR will be disconnected from {hostsCount} hosts in this pool. | Each shared SR will be disconnected from all hosts in this pool.
sr-disconnect-title: You are about to disconnect 1 SR | You are about to disconnect {n} SRs
start-console: Start your {type} to access the console and resume operations!
start-host: Start the host to resume operations, or check back later once it’s powered on.
start-vm: Start it to access its console, and configuration details.
template-has-bios-strings: The template already contains the BIOS strings
theme-dracula-description: A dark theme with vibrant colors
theme-monokai-description: Colorful theme inspired by code editors
theme-solarized-description: Precision colors for reduced eye strain
traffic-rule-delete-warning: This action is irreversible. The selected traffic rule will be permanently deleted.
traffic-rules:format-warning: Configuration error: Traffic rules are not in the expected format. Please {check-doc} on how to migrate them.
traffic-rules:info-message: Traffic rules order is being implemented. Behaviour may be hazardous.
unable-to-connect-to-the-pool: Unable to connect to the pool
unable-to-connect-to-xo-server: Unable to connect to XO server.
unable-to-create-new-bonded-network: Unable to create new bonded network
unable-to-create-new-internal-network: Unable to create new internal network
unable-to-create-new-traffic-rule: Unable to create new traffic rule
unable-to-migrate-vdi: Unable to migrate VDI on SR
untranslated-text-helper: By default, untranslated texts will be presented in english.
vdi-connect-title: You are about to connect 1 VDI | You are about to connect {n} VDIs
vdi-delete-warning: This will permanently delete the selected VDI and all data stored on it. This action cannot be undone.
vdi-detach-info: This will detach the selected VDI from the VM. The disk will not be deleted and can be reattached later.
vdi-detach-title: You are about to detach 1 VDI | You are about to detach {n} VDIs
vdi-disconnect-info: The VM will no longer have access to the VDI. The disk remains attached and you can reconnect later.
vdi-disconnect-title: You are about to disconnect 1 VDI | You are about to disconnect {n} VDIs
vdi-on-different-sr-warning: This SR is local to a host that doesn't hold the VM's other disks. The VM won't be able to start until all its disks are on the same host or on shared storage.
vif-connect-title: You are about to connect 1 VIF | You are about to connect {n} VIFs
vif-disconnect-info: The VM will no longer have access to the network. The VIF remains attached and you can reconnect it later.
vif-disconnect-title: You are about to disconnect 1 VIF | You are about to disconnect {n} VIFs
vm-backup-failure-number-of-retries: Number of retries if VM backup fails
vm-disable-blocked-protection: To proceed, you can disable the protection in the VM’s configuration.
vm-disable-delete-protection: To remove it, please edit its configuration and disable the deletion protection.
vm-off: This virtual machine is powered off.
vm-protected-deletion: This VM is currently protected against deletion.
vm-protected-force-reboot: This VM is protected against force reboot.
vm-protected-force-shutdown: This VM is protected against force shutdown.
vm-protected-pause: This VM is protected against pause.
vm-protected-reboot: This VM is protected against reboot.
vm-protected-shutdown: This VM is protected against shutdown.
vm-protected-suspend: This VM is protected against suspend.
warning:vdi-already-attached: The selected VDI is already attached to this VM.
warning:vdi-sr: This SR is not compatible with other VDIs' SRs. The SR needs to be shared or on the same host as the other VDIs' SRs.
what-does-protected-mean-content: A VM is considered protected when it’s included in at least one scheduled backup job and the last three runs of that job completed successfully. This helps ensure the VM has recent, reliable recovery points.
